title: DayStarter Labs

Observed good latency on small prompts; ran into rate limit errors during parallel test harness on 2026-03-13. No AE t...
text:
Inbound PLG signup. Solo founder (iOS dev) experimenting with chat + semantic search for a tiny customer service automation in-app. Created account 2026-03-01, used free credits to run prompt tests. Observed good latency on small prompts; ran into rate limit errors during parallel test harness on 2026-03-13. No AE touch yet — flagged as inbound-growth. Wants hosted API only (public cloud).

Shorthand from signup form: "early prototype, budget tiny, need quick integration into iOS app, prefer predictable per-token pricing, no enterprise SSO now".

Key quotes from in-app feedback form: "I need embeddings + chat that just work — low ops, low cost. If the demo shows <100ms p95 for short prompts I'm sold."

Asks: sample iOS SDK snippet, client-side key rotation guidance, basic cost calc for 100k tokens/mo. Interested in benching Redwood-hosted model vs third-party hosted endpoints (cost/la...

text:
Discovery notes (concise):
- Inbound from content marketing campaign (signup form + demo request).
- Company: BryteBridge — SMB marketing platform that auto-generates campaign content (multi-tenant SaaS for small brands).
- Primary ask: high-volume, low-cost text generation for daily newsletter + social + landing page variants.
- Volume: estimated 8M tokens/month in steady state; initial POC target = 1.2M tokens/month.
- Latency/throughput: p50 target <250ms for single prompt flows used by editor UI; bulk batch jobs run asynchronously (throughput 200-400 reqs/sec during batch windows).
- Cost sensitivity: high. Current vendor spend ~ $2.5k/month; want better unit economics at scale.
- Technical preference: open-models/quantized variants acceptable; interested in Redwood's model catalog + quantization guidance.
- Critical features called out: prefix/KV caching for template-heavy conten...

title: BridgeFlux Support Systems
preview:
Lead source: inbound via product signup (self-serve). Company builds "FluxAgent" — an agent layer for small-mid market support teams. Wants to integrate Redwood hosted API during a 10-day POC.

Key requirements (verbatim & shorthand captured):
- Workloads: high volume short-text ticket summarization + structured JSO...
text:
Lead source: inbound via product signup (self-serve). Company builds "FluxAgent" — an agent layer for small-mid market support teams. Wants to integrate Redwood hosted API during a 10-day POC.

Key requirements (verbatim & shorthand captured):
- Workloads: high volume short-text ticket summarization + structured JSON outputs for CRM ingestion. Also direct tool-calls to update ticket status in their system (webhook-based).
- Latency/throughput: aiming for ~150-250ms p95 for single-turn summary calls; expected concurrency 5-15 qps during peak; bursty behavior from shared inbox.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB — "cost per agent" matters; wants numbers for 7B quantized vs 13B hosted variants. Asked: "Can we run cheap open models for summaries and fallback to higher-quality for escalations?"
- Model preference: open-model friendly; prefers hosted open models in Redwood catalog (7B quantized for ma...

title: Brighton Helix Chat
preview: Inbound SMB demo lead from Product Hunt link — lightweight B2B chat SDK maker. Early-stage (pre-series A), 60 engineers+product. Want to embed a streaming assistant into their product (in-app chat + contextual suggestions). Key asks: low token-latency (p50 ~100ms), streaming partial responses to keep UX snappy, conf...
text:
Inbound SMB demo lead from Product Hunt link — lightweight B2B chat SDK maker. Early-stage (pre-series A), 60 engineers+product. Want to embed a streaming assistant into their product (in-app chat + contextual suggestions). Key asks: low token-latency (p50 ~100ms), streaming partial responses to keep UX snappy, configurable fallback routing when quota hits. Prefer open/compatible model flavors (quantized Llama variants or other open LLMs) but will evaluate Redwood hosted first for speed. Cost sensitive — expects ~5k-20k ARR initially from hosted API usage; wants clear per-token breakdown.

Sales notes (shorthand):
- "We need to make the assistant feel instantaneous" — Priya (Dev Lead)
- Will demo in-app flow using small client app next week.
- Trial org already has API keys, devs started integration (websocket + streaming). Observed ~140-180ms p50 in initial tests (non-optimized). Wan...

text: Inbound PLG lead from indie dev + micro-startup. Founder/CTO (Elliot Park) building a tiny multi-tenant coding assistant for indie SaaS customers. Signed up via docs and quickstart; no AE outreach yet. Key points: prefers hosted API (fast time-to-market), will likely stick with public cloud. Current sandbox usage: experimenting with chat + embeddings for code search. Quote from call: "We need something that 'just works' — latency under ~250ms p50 for short chats, and simple token billing we can pass to customers." Budget: very cost-sensitive; target < $1k/mo initially, with willingness to ramp if product-market fit. Wants to test smaller open models first (Llama-family, distilled) but may use Redwood-verified models if cost/perf is right. Security: expects basic audit logs and 30d retention; not asking for VPC or private deployment now. Operational concerns raised: rate limits per sub...

text:
Quick inbound trial signup (self-serve) — CRM lead from organic blog post on quick prompt guides.

Firmographics: SMB helpdesk SaaS, ~120 employees, primarily NA HQ, 30k monthly active support conversations. Looking to add conversational assistant and fast summarization for agent inbox.

Initial ask: "We signed up and love the simplicity. Production concern: responses feel slow for EU customers — we see 800-1,200 ms extra lag compared to US. Is there an easy region config?" — spoken by Head of Product on 2026-03-05 demo call.

What we've done so far (timeline):
- 2026-02-25: Self-serve signup, free credit used.
- 2026-03-02: Auto-instrumented latency metrics show p50 p95 differences by region; user reported EU p95 ~1.8s vs US p95 ~650ms.
- 2026-03-05: Discovery call (Fireflies ff_20260305_8923). Demo of hosted_api, basic SDK usage.
- 2026-03-08: Customer tried switching region param i...

text:
Inbound from product-led signup. Sees immediate value in embeddings for semantic search and chat assistant for merchant support.

Primary contact: CTO - Maya Patel (also handles infra/security). Quote: 'We need SOC2 evidence before procurement but can trial hosted API for 30 days.'

Latency/throughput: target p95 < 300ms for short chat turn, expect ~40-60 concurrent agents during business hours.

Model preference: cost-conscious; prefer quantized open models or small LLMs for initial deployment. Will test 2-3 variants.

Routing/fallback: interested in automatic fallback to cheaper model when load spikes; want easy pinning per API key.

Data handling: transactional data may include last4 PAN and customer notes => ask for redaction guidance and retention controls.

Security asks: SSO (SAML) integration, SOC2 report (type II) timeline, audit logs, KMS integration for key control. Data re...

title: BerryTrail AI
preview:
Lead source: inbound (community meetup & blog).
Summary: small ecommerce platform (~120 employees) building conversational product discovery + support assistant. Goal is to replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranker, then add a chat layer for product guidance. Very cost conscious but prioritizes non-h...
text:
Lead source: inbound (community meetup & blog).
Summary: small ecommerce platform (~120 employees) building conversational product discovery + support assistant. Goal is to replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranker, then add a chat layer for product guidance. Very cost conscious but prioritizes non-hallucinating answers for SKU/spec info.
What we heard on 3/9 call:
- "Customer quote: 'We need reliable answers about SKUs and specs — hallucinations cost us returns.'"
- Current infra: frontend + search on AWS; wants hosted_api (public cloud) to prototype quickly, then evaluate Dedicated later if traffic grows.
- POC scope: embeddings for 1M product text tokens (catalog + specs + reviews), reranker for top-10, chat for post-search follow-up. Expected POC volume: ~50k queries/month.
- Latency targets: embeddings batch job OK to be minutes; chat interactions need p95 <= 250m...

text:
Prospect inbound from self-serve trial (signed up via quickstart). Early discovery call 2026-03-02. Primary product interest: hosted API for call-summary + downstream lead scoring. Key ask: PII handling and retention. Wants option to redact/scrub PII at ingest and configurable retention windows (30/90/365 days).

Call snippets (rep paraphrase): "We need to be sure transcripts with customer emails/cc numbers do not leak into embeddings or long-term storage." Said they will test with 1000 weekly calls. Cost sensitivity moderate — SMB budget. Latency target: median generation <800ms for 1.2k token conversations; willing to accept up to 1.5s for batched summaries.

Security: requested SOC2 report, KMS support, audit logs; asks about retention controls exposed in Console (UI + API). Legal sent initial questionnaire (attached on Drive).

Notes on integration: prototyping with webhooks + Red...

Performance target: median chat latency <250ms and 95th <800ms from East US. Pricing ask: sample monthly invoices at 10k, 50k, 200k monthly requests and comparisons to OpenAI/Anthropic. Interested in guidance for batching/quantization tradeoffs to reduce token cost.

text:
Inbound SMB lead from wellness network. Wants to use Redwood Hosted API for: intake form parsing -> summarize -> populate internal CRM + support bot for members. HIPAA-adjacent — not full covered entity but handles sensitive PII/PHI-like fields. Key quotes: "We need to be 90% sure PHI never enters logs / long-term storage" and "Is hosted an option if we run a de-id step client-side?". 
Short summary: customer prefers low-friction hosted route (self-serve), cost-sensitive, wants guidance on retention and minimal logs. Interested in token-level cost breakdowns and examples of prefix/KV caching to reduce cost. SE call covered: sdk, streaming, how to implement client-side de-id (strip names, DOB, MRNs) before send, pros/cons. Customer concerns: server-side logs (access/audit), backups, S3/object retention for attachments. Legal wants documented retention SLA and whether request/response b...

text:
Inbound from self-serve signup — product team (CTO Ari K.) signed up for trial on 2026-03-02. Wants hosted API for production pilot (customer support bot + product search embeddings).

Key points from discovery call (2026-03-09 with Jordan + Maya):
- Use-case: chat-based support for mid-ticket inquiries (order status, returns), and embeddings for site search + facet-aware rerank.
- Latency need: p95 < 200ms for chat; search latency target < 120ms.
- Throughput: small initial footprint — ~30 qps peak for search, ~5 concurrent chat sessions; expects growth to ~200 qps in 6–9 months.
- Model preference: open-ish models (cost-aware) but wants Redwood-verified performance notes; fine with a mid-sized LLaMA/Mistral variant if latency/cost tradeoff is good.
- Security: SOC2 required; SSO (SAML) for internal console access; audit logs and data retention controls are a gating item for procurem...

text:
Inbound from webinar signup (Mar 2). Founder/CTO: Maya Ortiz (quote: 'we need cheap tokens at scale — think 3–5M tokens/day initially').

Discovery call 2026-03-03 (Jordan + Priya). Key points:
- Small engineering team (mobile + CMS integrations).
- Primary workload: high-throughput marketing content (email subject lines, ad variants, short blog drafts).
- Cost sensitive: wants to compare hosted cost vs self-host + open models. Leaning hosted for faster time-to-value.
- Interested in batching + prefix/KV caching for repeated templates to cut token spend. Asked about automatic caching suggestions.
- Latency target: median <200ms for short-turn generation (<=256 tokens). Will accept slightly higher for long-form.
- Model preferences: open/efficient models preferred (quantized variants ok). Not tied to GPT-family. Curious about Redwood model catalog and verified perf stats.
- Security: c...

text:
Lead: Blue Harbor Integrations — small dev tools shop building a docs-first developer portal. Primary ask: quick, drop-in examples to wire Redwood hosted API into their existing Next.js site using Vercel AI SDK and LangChain/LlamaIndex for retrieval. CTO quote: "We want a one-page quickstart to drop into Next.js and a copy-paste adapter for LangChain/LlamaIndex." Shorthand/CRM bullets:
- Using Next.js 14 + Vercel platform (deploys on Vercel).
- POC scope: connect docs (S3-hosted markdown) -> embeddings -> semantic search -> chat with context.
- Wants examples for both LangChain (agents + chains) and LlamaIndex (index + query) that show how to call Redwood hosted API with streaming and structured output.
- Concerned about cold-start latency and median token latency <150ms if possible; willing to accept bursty throughput.
- Billing: wants clear mapping of tokens -> estimated monthly cos...

title: Cobalt Nimbus LLC
preview:
Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signup on hosted API. Primary contact: Maria Lopez (CTO). Use case: conversational agent that calls internal tools (ticket search, run diagnostics) via function/tool-calls; need structured JSON output to map to internal RPC.

Summary of problem: In streaming + JSON_MODE the model sometim...
text:
Inbound SMB lead. Self-serve signup on hosted API. Primary contact: Maria Lopez (CTO). Use case: conversational agent that calls internal tools (ticket search, run diagnostics) via function/tool-calls; need structured JSON output to map to internal RPC.

Summary of problem: In streaming + JSON_MODE the model sometimes emits function_call events in multiple chunks. The Node SDK handler receives first chunk containing 'function_call' and partial 'arguments' string; later chunks complete it. Customer's parser tries to JSON.parse on first visible chunk and fails with 'Unexpected token' errors. Also observed cases where model returns single quotes instead of double quotes inside arguments ("TypeError: Unexpected token '\' in JSON at position 0"). They attempted a server-side workaround to buffer until end_of_stream but that increases latency and complexity.

Customer also reports that when...

Summary bullets:

Latency target: sub-300ms tail for chat responses to keep UX tight. Cost sensitivity: moderate — want predictable pay-as-you-go now, may consider Dedicated only if traffic > sustained 100k tokens/day.
- Model preference: open to Redwood catalog...

text:
Lead: CodeRipple Labs — small devtools startup building an in-editor AI pair programmer (VS Code + web) focused on whole-file refactors and long context code search.

Stage: discovery — early inbound from founder (CTO ex-small-OSS biz). Self-serve sign-up completed, played with REST endpoint.

Primary asks / pain: 
- Long context windows: need 50k+ token window for whole-repo retrieval + code-aware summarization. Quote: "We need entire file + call graph preface for accurate suggestions."
- Rate limits: unpredictable bursts from IDE telemetry + background indexing; want clear per-key burst policy and ability to request higher short-term bursts.
- SDKs: wants first-class JS/TS SDK with streaming chunk events, reconnect and auth refresh hooks; also Python SDK for backend batch reranking.
- Latency: interactive suggestions target ~150-300ms token latency for short completions; tolerate hi...

title: CodeQuiver Labs
preview: Lead: small devtools startup building AI code assistant that indexes user repos and provides multi-file suggestions. Main asks: 1) reliable long-context (40k+ tokens) behavior and cost estimates; 2) clear rate-limit semantics and graceful retry patterns; 3) mature SDKs (TS/Go/Python) with streaming + reconnect examp...
text: Lead: small devtools startup building AI code assistant that indexes user repos and provides multi-file suggestions. Main asks: 1) reliable long-context (40k+ tokens) behavior and cost estimates; 2) clear rate-limit semantics and graceful retry patterns; 3) mature SDKs (TS/Go/Python) with streaming + reconnect examples. Tech stack: Node backend, Rust worker for indexing, Postgres. Current approach: OpenAI + self-hosted embedding store; testing Redwood on sandbox for latency/cost comparisons. Quote from CTO: "We need consistent low-latency completions for live coding sessions — 200ms token latency target falling back to cheaper model when needed." Security: wants SOC2 + audit logs; SSO likely later. Notes shorthand: POC scope = 2wk sandbox -> measure token cost, latency profile, rate-limit impact under 5 concurrent sessions. Action items: provide sample SDK snippet that shows streaming...

text:
Interested in embedded product recommendation engine + help center search.

Primary constraint: cannot send PII to third parties without signed DPA.

CTO quote: "We need to be able to prove data is not used for model training — that's non-negotiable for compliance."

Cost sensitivity: early-stage margins, prefers hosted but wants conservative quotas.

Prefers multi-region (US + EU) but will start US-only for POC.

Asked for sample DPA and SOC2 Type II report excerpt.

AE action: consolidate answers, attach SOC2 redacted report, list infra providers (AWS, GCP via partner), provide DPA template.

text:
Lead source: docs quickstart -> self-serve signup. Small B2B SaaS (SaaS analytics for agencies) building embedded chat assistant in their product. Fast follow: want to test streaming endpoint with low client-side latency.

Key quotes: "Must feel instant on mobile, under 100ms ideally for short messages".

Tech stack: React Native front-end, Node backend, using hosted Redis for session state. Prefer public cloud — not ready for VPC/private. Cost sensitive — early freemium model, budget for hosted API likely < $10k ARR first year.

Model preferences: leaning toward smaller Llama-like model variants or Redwood-curated low-latency models; quantized runtime OK if latency preserved. Interested in streaming + partial responses to render tokens as they arrive.

Ops/security: wants SSO for admin portal later; for now needs audit logs and basic retention controls. Will ask for SOC2 mention in s...

text:
Primary contacts: Marcus Lee (CEO) and Olivia Park (CTO). Inbound via developer blog -> signup -> quick call. SUMMARY: SMB fintech building a transaction-aware customer support assistant + embeddings-powered search over transactions. Wants hosted_api to move fast; VPC is 'nice to have' later once production commitment made. SECURITY: asking for SOC2 report and SAML SSO guidance before POC; wants KMS + audit log examples; US-only data residency request. PERFORMANCE TARGETS: chat latency p50 <=200-300ms (short prompts), embeddings throughput ~150-250 reqs/min for POC. COST SENSITIVITY: budget-conscious, target <$0.02/token effective for gen; POC budget ~$8k-12k/mo. MODEL PREFERENCES: prefer small/medium open models (Llama2-like / Redwood-verified) with quantized runtimes; interested in batching and prefix caching tips. RECENT TIMELINE:

text:
Shorthand notes from calls and threads:
- Company: Aurora Bridge Search, small legaltech startup building client-facing contract search + internal research.
- Primary ask: replace brittle keyword search with embeddings + reranking pipeline; want hosted API (self-serve) to move fast.
- Latency target: interactive search ~<300ms (p95) on 1-10 qps baseline; may scale to 30-50 qps during peak. Cost sensitive — want guidance on tokens vs embedding costs.
- Model preference: open to small/efficient text-embedding models; asked about support for OpenAI-style embeddings + local quantized alternatives.
- Vector DB question: currently evaluating Pinecone and Postgres (pgvector); asked whether Redwood hosted API has first-class connectors or recommended adapter patterns.
- Reranking: customer expects two-stage pipeline: ANN candidate retrieval -> rerank with cross-encoder/generation signal. Want...

text:
Inbound via doc-search blog post; signed up for free tier and created first embedding index. Quick notes from discovery call: 
- CTO (Maya Singh) is primary technical contact; wants hosted API to avoid ops overhead.
- Primary workload: legal document search + contract Q&A (RAG). Low QPS (10-30 reqs/min) but need fast reranking for top-k (p95 latency target 150-250ms).
- Currently evaluating vector DBs: Pinecone + Weaviate; asking about recommended approach for reranking pipeline (embeddings -> ANN -> rerank via Hosted API LM).
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, expects usage to start small; asked about token pricing + embedding unit definitions.
- Model preference: open models preferred (Llama2 style), but open to Redwood-curated models if latency/cost are better.
- Security: requires SSO for dashboard, audit logs for queries, and ability to set retention to 90 days for PII. Not on-prem;...

text:
Lead source: inbound via SEO post 'best practices for RAG with small teams' and demo notebook.
Early-stage SMB: productized search for customer success docs and SOPs; want 2–3s p95 query latency, batch indexing overnight.
Trial behavior: has pushed ~12k docs, created embeddings via hosted endpoint (~1536-dim), ~2k vectors/day. Saw initial vector-store mismatch issues (duplicate IDs, inconsistent chunking).
Customer quote: "We want a drop-in hosted API — no infra to manage. Main questions: how to wire to our DB (Pinecone/Weaviate), cost per 1000 embed calls, and reranker recommendations."
Security: SOC2 important, SSO via SAML for admin console, audit logs for index operations, 90-day retention policy requested (can discuss retention controls).
Technical concerns noted during call: chunking strategy for PDFs, text normalization, on-the-fly reranking vs hybrid recall, small-batch latenc...

text:
Inbound from sales enablement app maker. CTO (Maya Rivers) reached out via sales enablement Slack community — interested in Redwood hosted API for call summarization and search overlay.
- Prefers self-serve trial first; wants to validate accuracy and cost vs AssemblyAI + OpenAI.
- Main concerns: PII in transcripts (names, emails, credit-card like strings). Asked: 'Can we redact PII before storing? How long do transcripts live in your system?'
- Wants control over retention (30/90/never persist) and ability to disable logs for specific routes.
- Use-case: Chrome/Zoom connector collects transcript -> stream to Redwood for real-time summary + finalize summary stored in customer's DB. Also embeddings for search (vector store in customer infra later).
- Latency: soft requirement 300-800ms for streaming sentence-level summaries; cost-sensitive — target <$0.01 per processed minute.
- Securit...

title: Elm Harbor Assistants

Observed behavior (customer reports / internal test...
text:
Inbound SMB lead via self-serve (free credits). Small engineering team (2 backend, 1 frontend). Primary product: chat-based support assistant + doc search across help center. Key pain: perceived slowness on user-facing chat — several customer complaints this week.

Observed behavior (customer reports / internal testing):
- Users in US experiencing ~600-900ms extra RTT vs EU users; account originally created with default region eu-west-1.
- Frontend shows request timeouts at ~12s (client-side) when prompts include long context + history (~1.5-3k tokens).
- Team tried increasing client timeout but worried about cost and UX.

What they've asked/said: 
- "We signed up fast, but users are saying the bot feels slow — is this something we can fix without moving infrastructure?" 
- Very cost-sensitive; prefer hosted API but may upgrade if clear perf wins.

Redwood quick wins suggested on call...
